articles.title: From non-league to Champions League - Raya's fairytale rise
articles.summary: Twelve years ago Arsenal keeper David Raya made his debut for non-league Southport; on Saturday he plays in the Champions League final - this is how he got there.

articles.title: Konate set to leave Liverpool on a free transfer
articles.summary: Ibrahima Konate is set to leave Liverpool for free when his current contract expires in June.

articles.title: Torreense to play European football while in second division
articles.summary: Portuguese Cup winners Torreense will join an exclusive group of second division clubs to play in European competitions after suffering play-off final defeat.

articles.title: How a pair of former Man Utd team-mates became firefighters
articles.summary: BBC Sport meets former professional footballers Abbie McManus and Jess Sigsworth to learn about their transition to becoming firefighters.

articles.title: Bristol City edging towards Skubala appointment
articles.summary: Bristol City are close to appointing Lincoln boss Michael Skubala as their new manager.

articles.title: 'We're just humans' - how Littler's tears revealed impact of untold struggle
articles.summary: Luke Littler’s teary on-stage interview after Premier League Darts glory provided a public reminder of the untold struggles sports’ biggest stars often face away from their arenas.

articles.title: Outrage at French Open after claims a match needed to be 'umpired by a man'
articles.summary: Adolfo Daniel Vallejo has provoked outrage by claiming his French Open match "needed to be umpired by a man" with sufficient "strength" to go against the Roland Garros crowd.

articles.title: Hodgkinson targets 800m world record on home soil
articles.summary: Olympic 800m champion Keely Hodgkinson has not ruled out the possibility of breaking athletics' longest-standing world record at the London Diamond League meeting in July.

articles.title: 'Step forward' for Littler as Premier League champion reveals doubts
articles.summary: Sky Sports Darts pundit Wayne Mardle hailed Luke Littler's honesty and talent after his thrilling 11-10 victory over Luke Humphries in the Premier League final.

articles.title: Hussain questions England 'changing winning formula' in India loss
articles.summary: Nasser Hussain questioned England's selection after Charlotte Edwards' side suffered a disappointing 38-run defeat to India in the opening match of their T20 series on Thursday night.

articles.title: Eze shrugs off Arsenal critics as he targets 'special' Champions League victory
articles.summary: "We are just another group of people that have the opportunity to win the Champions League and I pray that we take it. When we do, it will be a special moment for sure."

articles.title: Rice, Shaw and McInnes among BBC Football Awards winners
articles.summary: For the first time, BBC Sport is handing out end-of-season football awards to the cream of the crop in English and Scottish football - and here are the winners.

articles.title: Covid era gave Arteta space to revive Arsenal, says Kroenke
articles.summary: Arsenal co-chair Josh Kroenke says behind-closed-doors football during the Covid-19 pandemic allowed manager Mikel Arteta "space" that helped to revive the "sleeping giant".

articles.title: Palace wait on Iraola with Lampard among plan Bs
articles.summary: Crystal Palace are waiting on an answer from Andoni Iraola but Coventry boss Frank Lampard could be a plan B for the Conference League winners.

articles.title: How nine teams in Europe affects Premier League - and maybe Rochdale
articles.summary: For the second season in a row, the Premier League will have nine teams in Europe - and that will have knock-on effects domestically.

articles.title: The Sunderland footballer setting record straight after 46 years
articles.summary: When Roly Gregoire quit football in 1980 after suffering a knee injury, he had faced so much racism that he could not bear to watch football for many years.

articles.summary: Anthony Gordon's goal return in Europe coupled with his speed, aggression and work ethic suggests there is a lot more to come from the England international.

articles.summary: Brazil forward Neymar is set to be out for "two to three weeks" and means he will miss friendly matches against Panama and Egypt.
